title: Likelihood Function Construction in State-Space Modeling
description: A visual breakdown of the steps and components involved in building the likelihood function for a state-space model, illustrating the flow from observed data through latent states to the final probability calculation.
elements: System Matrices (Z_t, H_t, T_t, R_t, Q_t)
relationships: The latent state (α_t) evolves via the state equation and is observed through the measurement equation to produce (y_t)
The prediction error (v_t) is the difference between (y_t) and its forecast given (α_t)
The likelihood for a single observation is calculated using the Gaussian density with mean 0 and variance (F_t)
The total log-likelihood is the sum of the individual log-density contributions across all time points (t=1 to T)
